script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'pass7-confirm2',
  description: 'Pass-7: confirm the consolidated operator-identity PII remediation (J-002 concern + J-008)',
  phases: [{ title: 'Confirm' }],
}

const REPO = '/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay'

const SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  additionalProperties: false,
  required: ['verdict', 'appropriate', 'complete', 'rationale', 'concerns'],
  properties: {
    verdict: { type: 'string', enum: ['CONFIRMED', 'CONCERN', 'REJECTED'] },
    appropriate: { type: 'boolean' },
    complete: { type: 'boolean' },
    rationale: { type: 'string' },
    concerns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' } },
  },
}

const DESC = `Consolidated operator-identity PII remediation on branch harden/pass-7 (latest commit on the branch — read scripts/capture_transcripts.py as committed), addressing BOTH:
(a) J-002 confirm-concern: the prior full-name scrub ("[REDACTED]") left the operator's BARE first/last name in cleartext governance prose (256x "[REDACTED] approves ...", "[REDACTED] builds and governs ...").
(b) J-008 (new, caught by the pass-7 privacy coverage matrix): the operator GitHub handle "[REDACTED]" leaked across 193 transcript files (github.com/<handle> URLs, "gh --repo <handle>/..." commands) — PII no email rule (needs @) or username rule (home dir is "[REDACTED]") covered.

THE FIX (scripts/capture_transcripts.py):
1. _display_name_patterns(): in addition to the full-name pattern, now also emits a per-TOKEN pattern for each name token with len>=4 (e.g. "[REDACTED]\\b", "[REDACTED]\\b") — NO leading \\b (handles JSON-\\n-glued names), trailing \\b (protects "Joneses"). So the bare first/last name is redacted standalone.
2. _repo_handle_patterns() (NEW): derives the repo owner from "git remote -v" (regex github.com[:/]<owner>/) + the ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES env; redacts each handle (len>=4) as a bare token everywhere (URLs, gh args, <handle>@ email stem). Repo name is preserved (legitimate provenance).
3. Both applied in _scrub after the username rule.
4. The committed corpus was RE-SCRUBBED in place.
5. ROUND-2 FIX (this is the current state): an earlier round of this remediation was CASE-SENSITIVE and a prior confirmation found lowercase residue ("[REDACTED]@hcgrc", "[REDACTED]-[REDACTED] (human)", lowercase "[REDACTED]", and the handle stem "[REDACTED]" in "[REDACTED]-audience-profiles"). The current code adds re.IGNORECASE to the name-token AND handle patterns, and emits the handle STEM (handle minus a leading "the"). The corpus was re-scrubbed again. Your job is to confirm this round-2 state is now actually clean.
Regression tests: test_scrub_redacts_bare_name_tokens (incl. lowercase actor tokens), test_scrub_redacts_github_handle (incl. handle stem) (in tests/test_scripts.py).`

function prompt(lens) {
  return `You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer ${lens}) for the hc-assay production-hardening campaign, repo at ${REPO} (branch harden/pass-7).

${DESC}

Read the actual changed file ${REPO}/scripts/capture_transcripts.py and the tests. You may run tests: cd ${REPO} && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_scripts.py  (use "python -m pytest", NOT bare pytest).

CRITICALLY, independently verify the COMMITTED CORPUS is actually clean — run grep yourself, CASE-INSENSITIVELY this time (the prior round's gap was case-sensitivity), e.g.:
  cd ${REPO} && grep -riwl "[REDACTED]" transcripts/ | wc -l       # word-boundary '[REDACTED]', any case
  cd ${REPO} && grep -ril "[REDACTED]" transcripts/ | wc -l   # handle + stem, any case
  cd ${REPO} && grep -rioE "[REDACTED]([^e]|$)" transcripts/ | sort -u   # standalone '[REDACTED]' (NOT the protected 'joneses')
  cd ${REPO} && grep -rioE "[REDACTED][@-]" transcripts/ | sort -u  # actor-token forms [REDACTED]@host / [REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
A passing unit test does NOT prove the committed transcripts are clean — verify both. NOTE: a coincidental '[REDACTED]' SUBSTRING inside a random alphanumeric token (no word boundary, e.g. '...JVThoMaSg2...') is NOT a leak and is correctly left alone; and the bare provider word 'protonmail' / scrub-meta-discussion of 'Joneses' are not PII. Distinguish a real operator-identity leak from these.

Decide:
- appropriate: right root cause, right layer, sound approach, acceptable over-redaction trade-off (the corpus has no unrelated bearer of "[REDACTED]"/"[REDACTED]"; confirm or refute by sampling contexts).
- complete: corpus actually clean (grep ~0 for operator name tokens + handle, ignoring the bare provider word "protonmail" in meta-discussion which is not PII), regression tests discriminate (would fail if reverted), and no obvious remaining operator-identity vector.
Return CONFIRMED only if both true. CONCERN if appropriate but a real residual gap remains (name a concrete one). REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns; empty array if none.`
}

phase('Confirm')
const votes = (await parallel([
  () => agent(prompt('A'), { label: 'confirm:privacy:A', phase: 'Confirm', schema: SCHEMA }).then((v) => ({ lens: 'A', ...v })),
  () => agent(prompt('B'), { label: 'confirm:privacy:B', phase: 'Confirm', schema: SCHEMA }).then((v) => ({ lens: 'B', ...v })),
])).filter(Boolean)

const bothConfirmed = votes.length === 2 && votes.every((v) => v.verdict === 'CONFIRMED')
log(`PRIVACY REMEDIATION (J-002 concern + J-008): ${bothConfirmed ? 'BOTH-CONFIRMED' : 'NEEDS-ATTENTION'} — ${votes.map((v) => v.lens + ':' + v.verdict).join(', ')}`)
return { bothConfirmed, votes }

votes.rationale: Round-2 remediation is correct and the committed corpus is verified clean. ROOT CAUSE/LAYER/APPROACH (appropriate): both J-002 (bare first/last name in cleartext) and J-008 (GitHub handle) are addressed at the right layer — the _scrub seam in scripts/capture_transcripts.py, after the username rule. _display_name_patterns() emits per-token patterns (len>=4, trailing \b, no leading \b for JSON-\n-glued names, re.IGNORECASE) and _repo_handle_patterns() derives the owner from `git remote -v` (confirmed = [REDACTED], the actual operator) + ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES, redacting the handle and its stem (handle minus leading "the") case-insensitively. Over-redaction is bounded and acceptable: the len>=4 guard + trailing \b protect longer words (Joneses), and sampling shows the corpus has no unrelated bearer of these tokens. CORPUS CLEAN (complete): case-insensitive greps return 0 files for `[REDACTED]` (word-bound), `[REDACTED]` (word-bound), `[REDACTED]@`/`[REDACTED]-` forms, standalone `[REDACTED]`, full handle `[REDACTED]`, and handle stem `[REDACTED]`. Scrub demonstrably ran (1388 files contain [REDACTED]; [REDACTED]@protonmail shows the email local-part was redacted while the bare provider word protonmail — not PII — correctly remains). Non-leaks correctly left alone: the random base64 substring JVThoMaSg2ys (no word boundary) and third-party github.com owners (Arize-ai, langchain-ai, anthropics, etc., legitimate provenance). TESTS DISCRIMINATE: 13/13 pass; test_scrub_redacts_bare_name_tokens asserts lowercase [REDACTED]@hcgrc/[REDACTED]-[REDACTED] are redacted while 'joneses' survives (fails if IGNORECASE or trailing \b reverted), and test_scrub_redacts_github_handle asserts both handle and the stem-minus-"the" are gone while the repo name survives (fails if stem expansion reverted). Both true → CONFIRMED.

Independently verified both code and committed corpus on branch harden/pass-7 (HEAD fbb9511). The round-2 fix is appropriate: correct root cause (operator-identity PII distinct from email/username), correct layer (_scrub in capture_transcripts.py, applied after the username rule), sound approach (per-token name redaction with trailing \b + re.IGNORECASE; handle+stem redaction derived from git remote/env). Over-redaction is acceptable — sampled "[REDACTED]"/"[REDACTED]" contexts are all operator governance references and "Joneses" is correctly preserved. The two in-scope items are clean: grep -riwl "[REDACTED]" = 0 files, grep -ril "[REDACTED]" = 0 files, no [REDACTED]@/[REDACTED]- actor tokens, no standalone "[REDACTED]". Regression tests pass 13/13 and genuinely discriminate — I reproduced that a case-sensitive revert leaves "[REDACTED]@hcgrc" and a no-stem revert leaves "octocatx", exactly what the tests assert against. HOWEVER a closely-adjacent residual operator-identity vector remains: the human-readable brand persona "[REDACTED]"/"[REDACTED]" is in cleartext in ~20 files (e.g. "through [[REDACTED]](https://[REDACTED].substack.com)", "[REDACTED] persona taxonomy", "[REDACTED] audience profiles"). The scrubbed GitHub handle "[REDACTED]" is literally this brand de-spaced, and the operator email is [REDACTED] — so a reader trivially reconstructs the already-redacted handle and email by concatenation. This is the same J-008 vector, space-separated; the fix scrubs the de-spaced handle/slug-stem but not the spaced source brand it derives from. Therefore appropriate but not complete.
concerns: Residual operator-identity leak: the brand persona '[REDACTED]' / '[REDACTED]' appears in cleartext across ~20 transcript files (e.g. 'through [[REDACTED]](https://[REDACTED].substack.com)', '[REDACTED] persona taxonomy', '[REDACTED] audience profiles'). The scrubbed GitHub handle '[REDACTED]' is exactly this brand with spaces removed and the operator email is [REDACTED], so the redacted handle/email are trivially reconstructable by concatenation. The handle-stem rule covers de-spaced derivatives ([REDACTED]-audience-profiles) but not the spaced source brand. Fix: add a space-flexible pattern for the brand persona (e.g. derive from the handle by inserting optional separators, or add '[REDACTED]' to ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES/NAMES with whitespace-flexible matching) and re-scrub the corpus.

logs: PRIVACY REMEDIATION (J-002 concern + J-008): NEEDS-ATTENTION — A:CONFIRMED, B:CONCERN
summary: Pass-7: confirm the consolidated operator-identity PII remediation (J-002 concern + J-008)
